assert 0.0.1
  • Get started
  • Reference

Assert that at least one of several arguments is supplied

Source: R/arguments.R
assert_at_least_one.Rd

Treats a NULL argument as "not supplied". Raises an error if every argument in ... is NULL.

Usage

assert_at_least_one(..., call = rlang::caller_env())

Arguments

...

Two or more arguments to check, typically optional parameters of the calling function that default to NULL.

call

Environment used as the error's call context. Defaults to the calling function, so errors point at the user's code.

Value

NULL, invisibly.

Examples

fetch <- function(id = NULL, name = NULL) {
  assert_at_least_one(id, name)
  "ok"
}
fetch(name = "ada")
#> [1] "ok"
